engineGuide.h2: Aston Martin DB7 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Aston Martin DB7 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Original DB7 engine, Jaguar-derived AJ6 block with Eaton supercharger; defined the affordable Aston entry point.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - overheating can push cylinder liners, common on neglected cooling systems.
Supercharger bearing wear - whining noise from Eaton M62 unit after 70k miles.
Timing chain guide wear - rattling on cold start, requires specialist inspection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM1 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Revised AM1 with minor reliability updates; most common straight-six code found in UK DB7s.
compatibleVariants: DB7 3.2 Coupe, DB7 3.2 Volante, DB7 3.2 Automatic
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - overheating can push cylinder liners, common on neglected cooling systems.
Supercharger bearing wear - whining noise from Eaton M62 unit after 70k miles.
ECU sensor faults - lambda sensor and MAF issues triggering management light.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM2 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production I6 with refined supercharger calibration; transitional code before V12 dominance.
commonFailures: Oil consumption increase - valve stem seal wear on higher-mileage supercharged units.
Intercooler pipe cracking - boost pressure leaks affecting performance and economy.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling on cold start, requires specialist inspection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM3 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Final straight-six iteration, fitted to badged Vantage models before full V12 transition.
commonFailures: Oil consumption increase - valve stem seal wear on higher-mileage supercharged units.
Intercooler pipe cracking - boost pressure leaks affecting performance and economy.
ECU sensor faults - lambda sensor and MAF issues triggering management light.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM4 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Original DB7 V12 unit, naturally aspirated 60-degree V12; defined the Vantage era.
compatibleVariants: DB7 V12 Vantage Coupe, DB7 V12 Vantage Volante
commonFailures: Cam chain tensioner failure - causes top-end rattle and potential catastrophic valve damage from 60k miles.
Coil pack failure - misfires on cylinders 6/12 common due to heat exposure in V12 bay.
Rear main seal leak - oil seepage requiring engine-out service on high-mileage units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM V12 AM1 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Revised V12 with updated ECU mapping and minor reliability improvements; most common V12 code.
compatibleVariants: DB7 V12 Vantage, DB7 Vantage 6.0, DB7 V12 Volante
commonFailures: Cam chain tensioner failure - causes top-end rattle and potential catastrophic valve damage from 60k miles.
Coil pack failure - misfires on cylinders 6/12 common due to heat exposure in V12 bay.
Oil consumption increase - valve seal wear on higher-mileage naturally aspirated units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM V12 AM2 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

entries.title: 5.9L Petrol
entries.history: Final DB7 V12 code, fitted to GT/GTA and limited editions with performance calibration.
compatibleVariants: DB7 GT, DB7 GTA, DB7 Vantage 24, DB7 Vantage V600, DB7 Vantage Le Mans
commonFailures: Cam chain tensioner failure - causes top-end rattle and potential catastrophic valve damage from 60k miles.
Coil pack failure - misfires on cylinders 6/12 common due to heat exposure in V12 bay.
High-performance variant stresses - V600 supercharger kits require specialist rebuild knowledge.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AM V12 AM3 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Aston Martin DB7 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Aston Martin DB7's 3.2L supercharged I6 and 5.9L V12 engines are engineering classics, but age and mileage bring known failure points. Timing chain tensioners on the V12 and head gaskets on the straight-six dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: DB7 V12 Cam Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: DB7 V12 Cam Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Top-End Rattle Leads to Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB7 V12 Vantage, Vantage 6.0, GT, GTA (1999-2003, AM V12 AM1-AM3)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure over time, allowing chain slap that damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front covers, replace tensioners/guides, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before chain jump; 20-30k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; restores reliability for another 50k+ miles

problems.recommendation: On a DB7 V12 worth £25k-£45k, if you hear that rattle, get it inspected immediately. If guides are damaged, replacement is often more economical than repair. We recommend a rebuilt V12 with upgraded tensioners for long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AM V12 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB7

problems.h4: DB7 3.2L Head Gasket Failure - Overheating Risks on the Supercharged I6
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB7 3.2 Coupe/Volante, 3.2 Vantage (1994-2000, AM1-AM4)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder liner movement under heat stress compromises head seal; exacerbated by coolant neglect.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, pressure test cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if liners intact; 15-25k mile extension with careful monitoring

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt supercharged I6 with upgraded liners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; addresses root cause, restores performance

problems.recommendation: If your DB7 3.2 has overheated or shows coolant/oil mixing, don't risk a simple gasket job. Liner damage is common. A rebuilt AM2/AM4 engine with upgraded components is the smarter long-term investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AM2/AM4 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

problems.h4: DB7 V12 Ignition Coil Failure - Misfires That Mask Bigger Problems
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB7 V12 Vantage, Volante, GT (1999-2003, AM V12 AM1-AM3)
problems.rootCause: Heat exposure in tight V12 bay degrades coil insulation; cylinders 6/12 worst affected.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 12 coils and spark plugs, clear codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom; coils may fail again in 20-30k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded ignition components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution if misfires indicate deeper wear; restores full performance

problems.recommendation: If misfires persist after coil replacement, or the engine has 80k+ miles, consider a rebuilt V12. The labour to access coils is significant; investing in a full rebuild can be more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare AM V12 engine replacement prices for your DB7

problems.h4: DB7 3.2L Supercharger Bearing Failure - Whine That Signals End of Life
problems.rootCause: Eaton M62 supercharger bearings wear from heat and oil starvation; whine precedes seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, replace bearings/seals, re-fit and test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is sound; 20-30k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with refurbished supercharger, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; addresses supercharger and potential engine wear together

problems.recommendation: If supercharger whine is accompanied by oil consumption or performance loss, the engine may be tired. A rebuilt AM2/AM4 unit with refurbished supercharger offers the best value and reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt DB7 3.2L engine with supercharger

problems.h4: DB7 V12 Oil Leak - Rear Main Seal and Cam Cover Issues
problems.affectedModels: Aston Martin DB7 V12 variants (1999-2003, AM V12 AM1-AM3)
problems.rootCause: Age-hardened seals and gaskets; rear main seal requires engine removal to access.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cam cover gaskets, front seals, sump gasket; external access only
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses visible leaks; rear main seal requires engine-out

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with all new seals/gaskets, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; eliminates all leak points and addresses internal wear

problems.recommendation: If oil loss is significant or the rear main seal is suspect, factor in the labour to remove the engine. Often, a rebuilt V12 with all-new seals is more economical than repeated seal replacements.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt V12 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB7

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Aston Martin DB7
finalCta.paragraph: The DB7's engines are magnificent but complex. When failure strikes, repair costs can approach the car's value. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with transparent pricing, upgraded components addressing known weaknesses, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and keep your DB7 on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Aston Martin DB7 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Aston Martin DB7 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

families: AM1/AM2/AM3/AM4 - 3.2L supercharged I6 (AJ6 lineage), dominant in early DB7s, head gasket and supercharger concerns

foundIn: DB7 V12 Vantage (1999-2003) - 5.9L V12 (AM V12 AM1-AM3)
knownFor: Magnificent V12 soundtrack and linear power delivery in Vantage models
Supercharged I6 offers accessible Aston ownership with careful maintenance
Timing chain tensioner failure on V12s and head gasket risks on I6s drive replacement enquiries

engineYears.h2: Aston Martin DB7 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the DB7's 1994-2003 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The DB7 launched with the Jaguar-derived 3.2L supercharged inline-six, establishing the accessible Aston formula.
keyChanges: DB7 Coupe introduced with AM1 3.2L supercharged I6 (335hp)
Manual and automatic transmission options available
Early cooling system design later refined to address head gasket risks
knownFor: Accessible entry into Aston Martin ownership
Supercharger whine develops with bearing wear after 70k miles
Head gasket vulnerability if cooling system neglected
Strong enthusiast community and specialist support in UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1996 Aston Martin DB7 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB7 3.2 head gasket replacement • DB7 supercharger bearing failure • AM1 engine rebuild cost

years.preview: Mid-Production: Refined I6 and V12 Introduction
years.description: The AM2/AM3 codes brought minor I6 refinements, while 1999 marked the pivotal arrival of the 5.9L V12 Vantage.
keyChanges: AM2/AM3 engine codes introduced with incremental reliability updates
DB7 Volante convertible added to range (1996 onwards)
1999: DB7 V12 Vantage launched with new 5.9L naturally aspirated V12 (420hp)
mainEngines: AM V12 AM1 - 5.9L petrol naturally aspirated (DB7 V12 Vantage)
popularModels: Aston Martin DB7 V12 Vantage Coupe
Aston Martin DB7 V12 Vantage Volante
knownFor: V12 launch transformed DB7 into genuine performance GT
Timing chain tensioner concerns emerge on early V12 units
I6 models remain popular for lower running costs and simpler maintenance
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-1999 Aston Martin DB7 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB7 V12 timing chain rattle • DB7 3.2 supercharger rebuild • V12 coil pack failure fix

years.preview: Final Production: V12 Dominance and Limited Editions
years.description: The DB7's final years saw V12 engines dominate, with performance-focused GT, GTA and ultra-rare V600/Le Mans variants.
keyChanges: AM4 code for late I6 models (3.2 Vantage) before full V12 transition
AM V12 AM2/AM3 codes with updated ECU mapping and minor reliability refinements
Limited editions: V600 (600hp supercharged V12), Le Mans, Vantage 24, GT/GTA
mainEngines: AM V12 AM2/AM3 - 5.9L petrol naturally aspirated (Vantage 6.0, GT, GTA, V600, Le Mans)
popularModels: Aston Martin DB7 Vantage Le Mans
knownFor: V600 and Le Mans represent the ultimate DB7 performance, with supercharged V12 conversions
GT/GTA models highly sought after for balanced performance and usability
V12 tensioner and ignition coil failures dominate late-model replacement enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2003 Aston Martin DB7 replacement engines
years.ticker: DB7 V600 engine rebuild • DB7 GT V12 replacement • AM V12 AM3 tensioner upgrade

faq.h2: Aston Martin DB7 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a DB7 V12 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Aston Martin DB7 V12 engine typically costs £8,500-£14,500 supply-only in the UK, with supply & fit adding £1,800-£4,500 depending on variant and workshop. Prices vary by engine code (AM V12 AM1-AM3) and whether upgraded tensioners are fitted.
keyPoints: Rebuilt V12 prices range £8,500-£14,500 supply-only UK market average
Supply & fit labour adds £1,800-£4,500 depending on variant complexity
Upgraded cam chain tensioners recommended to prevent repeat failure
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard through EngineMarket specialists
items.cta: Compare rebuilt AM V12 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB7

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a DB7 3.2?
items.answer: Yes, replacing a DB7 3.2 engine is often worthwhile given the car's £15k-£35k value. A rebuilt AM2/AM4 engine costs £4,200-£7,800 supply-only, preserving your investment while addressing known head gasket or supercharger weaknesses.
keyPoints: DB7 3.2 values (£15k-£35k) justify £4,200-£7,800 rebuilt engine investment
Rebuilt units include upgraded liners and supercharger bearings where applicable
Supply & fit available nationwide through Aston Martin specialist workshops
12-month warranty protects your replacement investment
items.cta: Get quotes for AM2/AM4 Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement

items.question: What causes the DB7 V12 timing chain rattle and how do I fix it?
items.answer: DB7 V12 timing chain rattle stems from hydraulic tensioner failure allowing chain slap, potentially damaging guides and jumping timing. Fix requires tensioner/guide replacement (£2,200-£3,800 specialist) or full rebuilt engine (£8,500-£14,500) if damage is extensive.
keyPoints: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure after 60k-90k miles causing chain slap
Ignoring rattle risks valve damage from jumped timing chain
Specialist tensioner/guide replacement viable if caught early before damage
Rebuilt V12 with upgraded tensioners offers definitive long-term solution
items.cta: Compare rebuilt V12 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB7

items.question: Can I fit a V12 engine into a 3.2L DB7 chassis?
items.answer: Technically possible but prohibitively complex and costly. The DB7 3.2 and V12 share chassis architecture but differ in ECU, wiring, cooling and mounting points. A V12 conversion typically exceeds £25k+ in parts and labour, often surpassing the car's value.
keyPoints: V12 conversion requires ECU, wiring loom, cooling and mounting modifications
Labour costs typically exceed £15k plus V12 engine cost (£8,500-£14,500)
DVLA and insurance implications for major powertrain changes
Most owners opt for rebuilt 3.2L engine preserving originality and value
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt DB7 3.2L engine with supercharger

items.question: What is the warranty on a DB7 replacement engine from EngineMarket?
items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Aston Martin DB7 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Warranty terms are confirmed with your chosen supplier before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplying specialist workshop
Terms confirmed in writing before engine dispatch
Used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month warranties
items.cta: Find Aston Martin DB7 engine supply & fit deals with warranty

items.question: How long does a DB7 engine replacement take?
items.answer: A DB7 engine swap typically takes 3-5 working days for supply & fit at a specialist workshop, depending on variant complexity and parts availability. V12 models require additional time for transmission removal and ECU recalibration.
keyPoints: 3.2L I6 replacement: 3-4 days typical at specialist workshop
V12 Vantage replacement: 4-5 days due to transmission and calibration complexity
Engine supply-only orders dispatch within 1-3 days if in stock
Allow extra time for diagnostics and post-fit road testing
items.cta: Get quotes for Aston Martin DB7 engine replacement with turnaround estimate

items.question: Are DB7 V12 engines reliable?
items.answer: DB7 V12 engines are robust when maintained but have known failure points: cam chain tensioners (60k-90k miles) and coil packs (50k-85k miles). Proactive maintenance and rebuilt units with upgraded components significantly improve long-term reliability.
keyPoints: V12 block and internals are robust with proper oil and cooling maintenance
Tensioner and coil pack failures are predictable, not random
Rebuilt V12s with upgraded tensioners address the primary weakness
Regular oil changes with correct spec critical for V12 longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt AM V12 engine prices for your Aston Martin DB7

items.question: What is the difference between DB7 and DB7 Vantage engines?
items.answer: Early DB7s (1994-1999) used the 3.2L supercharged inline-six (AM1-AM4), while DB7 Vantage models (1999-2003) feature the 5.9L naturally aspirated V12 (AM V12 AM1-AM3). Power outputs differ: 335-340hp for I6 vs 420-435hp for V12.
keyPoints: DB7 3.2: supercharged I6, 335-340hp, AM1-AM4 codes, 1994-2000
DB7 Vantage: naturally aspirated V12, 420-435hp, AM V12 AM1-AM3, 1999-2003
V12 models require different transmission, cooling and ECU systems
Engine codes are non-interchangeable; verify VIN before ordering replacement
items.cta: Compare Aston Martin DB7 V12 Vantage engine prices

items.question: Do you have DB7 engines with matching numbers?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket specialists can source matching-numbers or correct-code Aston Martin DB7 engines for custodial restorations. Provide your VIN to verify the original engine code (AM1-AM4 or AM V12 AM1-AM3) before ordering.
keyPoints: Matching-numbers engines available for concours-level DB7 restorations
VIN check confirms original engine code and variant specification
Correct-code rebuilt engines preserve authenticity and resale value
Documentation and build sheets provided with remanufactured units
items.cta: Enquire about matching-numbers Aston Martin DB7 engine availability

items.question: Can I buy a DB7 engine online and fit it myself?
items.answer: While supply-only DB7 engines are available online, DIY fitment is strongly discouraged due to the DB7's complex electronics, transmission integration and calibration requirements. Professional supply & fit through Aston Martin specialists is recommended.
keyPoints: DB7 engine swaps require ECU recalibration and specialist diagnostic tools
V12 models need transmission removal and precise alignment procedures
Incorrect fitment can void warranty and cause costly secondary damage
EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists for supply & fit
items.cta: Find Aston Martin DB7 engine supply & fit deals nationwide
